title: Urostigma cestrifolium
intro: Urostigma cestrifolium, commonly known as the Indian fig, is a type of plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Urostigma cestrifolium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Urostigma cestrifolium is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and digestive issues.
